seoTitle: Mount Warning vs Mount Warrigal — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Mount Warning and Mount Warrigal. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Mount Warning vs Mount Warrigal: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Mount Warning and Mount Warrigal.
heading: Mount Warning vs Mount Warrigal — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Mount Warning, New South Wales and Mount Warrigal, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Mount Warning has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Mount Warrigal (46/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Mount Warning or Mount Warrigal safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Mount Warning has a flood risk score of 28/100, while Mount Warrigal scores 47/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Mount Warning scores 30/100 for bushfire risk. Mount Warrigal scores 56/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Mount Warning or Mount Warrigal?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Mount Warning has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.38% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
